image_tag_gc_state.reason: lost to artifact cleanup between ω-runner-7 and ω-runner-8
image_tag_gc_state.loc_note: ω-runner-7 closure quoted 123 LOC; re-derivation came in at 166 (added cross-platform date parsing + size-unit normalization comments + safer here-string read loop). All subcommands and sentinel format preserved.

build_runner_image_change.hook_location: after the '=== IMAGE OK ===' summary block, before the 'smoke test:' user-instruction block
hook_behavior: checks env HEXA_RUNNER_NO_AUTO_GC; if '1', prints 'auto-gc : disabled' and skips
else if tool/image_tag_gc.sh is missing, prints 'auto-gc : skip' (no fail)
else runs `bash <repo_root>/tool/image_tag_gc.sh prune`, prints captured stdout (sentinel)
non-zero gc exit only emits a warn line — build success is preserved

env_var_added.name: HEXA_RUNNER_NO_AUTO_GC
use_cases: CI builds where prune is owned externally
operator wants manual control during a recovery window

auto_prune_fires_on_success_path.method: shell-simulated the exact code path the hexa orchestrator now runs (file_exists check + bash-shell-out + stdout capture)

opt_out_disables_auto_prune.method: set HEXA_RUNNER_NO_AUTO_GC=1, replayed the env-check branch

insufficient_tags_no_op.method: live prune call against current real tag set (4 tags, keep_n=4)
insufficient_tags_no_op.verdict: PASS — no destructive action, sentinel still emitted

side_effect_observed.fact: Test 2 of the standalone script run (before insufficient-tags case) actually deleted hexa-runner:f9ccfa3d (count was 5, keep_n=4); subsequent calls now no-op at 4 tags
side_effect_observed.matches_omega_runner_7_dry_run: yes — kept=4 deleted=1 freed_mb=170 was exactly the projection

documentation.build_runner_image_header: added ω-runner-8 paragraph documenting auto-prune + HEXA_RUNNER_NO_AUTO_GC opt-out
documentation.image_tag_gc_header: added auto-prune integration section pointing back to build_runner_image.hexa

rollback.remove_header_paragraph: delete the ω-runner-8 paragraph in the file header comment
